mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month without requiring additional macroeconomic confirmation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings downgrades, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hazard ratio for negative returns post-threshold breach is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace consumption, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, but this positive effect on equity returns only materializes when the federal funds rate remains below 4.0 percent, as higher rates compress valuation multiples.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between GDP growth and forward returns disappears or reverses when FEDFUNDS is below 4.0 percent, or the regime-split Sharpe ratio difference is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp pullback in business and consumer confidence, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month as construction and related sectors drag on earnings.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative following housing start declines greater than 5 percent, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises alongside a tightening monetary stance, it indicates supply-side resilience that outpaces financial conditions, leading to positive equity returns over a quarter as earnings growth offsets discount rate pressures.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4 percent forces market expectations of accelerated policy tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative following CPI spikes greater than 0.4 percent, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
